title: U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft
summary: U.S. GSSAP satellites have conducted a handoff maneuver in geostationary orbit to monitor China's Shijian-29 spacecraft. Commercial space tracking data indicates these satellites are maintaining close proximity for continuous observation.

title: Space Command begins phased move to Alabama
summary: The U.S. Space Command has initiated a phased relocation to Alabama, with the new headquarters expected to be completed by 2031. This move marks a significant shift in the command's operational base.

title: Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space
summary: Government agencies adopting commercial procurement models for space capabilities may not directly benefit private companies. This approach has inherent limitations in expanding business opportunities for the space industry.

title: Xona raises $170 million for satellite navigation network
summary: Xona raises $170 million to expand its satellite navigation network, aiming to deploy a 258-satellite constellation for positioning, navigation, and timing services.

title: China sends radar mapping and GNSS augmentation sats into orbit with pair of launches
summary: China launched two satellites to enhance its SuperView and CentiSpace LEO constellations, including radar mapping and GNSS augmentation payloads. The pair of launches expands China's Earth observation and navigation capabilities.

title: Satlantis earnings grow alongside demand for Earth-observation satellites
summary: Satlantis reported revenues of 47.8 million euros in 2025, with over 50% of its income from small-satellite sales and operations. The Spanish company's earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization reached 14.4 million euros.

title: HTS Market Set to Reach $76B as Industry Enters Terabit Era
summary: The High Throughput Satellites (HTS) market is projected to reach $76 billion by 2034, with global demand expected to hit 218 Tbps. Novaspace's latest report highlights the industry's transition into the terabit era.

title: Hughes targets sovereign satcom demand with network control software
summary: Hughes is addressing sovereign satellite communications demand with new network control software. The company also introduced an AI-enabled modem to support militaries transitioning to hybrid networks.

title: Golden Dome and the velocity race: Why ground-based optics are the key to mission persistence
summary: The Golden Dome mission for missile defense faces challenges due to hypersonic glide vehicles and decoys, requiring precise ground-based optics for accuracy. The mission highlights the need for disciplined systems engineering under high-stakes conditions.

title: Hitting the brakes: Hubble Space Telescope watches doomed comet reverse its spin
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope observed comet 41P/Tuttle–Giacobini–Kresák's rotation slowing, reversing, and then speeding up, likely due to jet-like gas outbursts. This unprecedented behavior provides new insights into cometary dynamics.

title: Sun storms are powered by a magnetic engine 16 Earths deep, study finds
summary: A study has found that the Sun's powerful magnetic field, which drives solar storms, is generated about 16 Earths deep beneath its visible surface. This discovery challenges previous assumptions about the location of the Sun's magnetic engine.

title: 'For All Mankind' is confirmed for season 6, but it'll be the final mission for Apple TV's incredible space show
summary: The space drama series 'For All Mankind' has been confirmed for a sixth and final season on Apple TV+. The show's creators expressed excitement about concluding the story as intended.

title: Don't miss the waxing moon swing by the Beehive star cluster March 27
summary: The moon will pass by the Beehive star cluster on March 27-28, offering a celestial viewing opportunity. This event involves the moon's orbit and a distant star cluster, not directly related to the space industry.

title: Artemis 2 moon mission latest news: NASA prepares for April 1 launch for lunar astronauts
summary: NASA is preparing for the April 1 launch of the Artemis 2 moon mission, which will carry astronauts to the lunar orbit. This marks a significant milestone in the Artemis program aimed at returning humans to the Moon.

title: Rattling the Roman Space Telescope | Space photo of the day for March 26, 2026
summary: NASA released a new image showing the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope undergoing vibration testing to simulate launch conditions. The testing is part of preparations for the telescope's eventual deployment.

title: Incredible new NASA images reveal Saturn in a new light — and it's all thanks to a telescope team-up from Webb and Hubble
summary: NASA has released stunning new images of Saturn, showcasing its atmosphere and rings in unprecedented detail. The observations were made possible through a collaborative effort between the James Webb and Hubble space telescopes.

title: Who is Artemis? Meet the Greek goddess who inspired NASA's return to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis program draws its name from the Greek goddess Artemis, twin sister of Apollo, symbolizing a new era of lunar exploration beyond the Apollo missions. The program includes Artemis 2 and future missions aimed at returning humans to the Moon.

title: These 45 exoplanets may be the best places to search for alien life
summary: A new catalog identifies 45 exoplanets as potential candidates for hosting alien life, offering a framework for future observational studies. This discovery aims to guide the search for extraterrestrial life in the universe.

title: Aim for the stars for under $15 with this Estes 2441 Mini Arcas Model Rocket Kit at Walmart, now 17% cheaper in the spring sales
summary: Walmart is offering a 17% discount on the Estes 2441 Mini Arcas Model Rocket Kit, allowing enthusiasts to launch a mini-mission for under $15. The deal is positioned as a show of support for the Artemis 2 crew.

title: Satellite Spots a Spawn
summary: Satellite imagery detected the activity of herring around Vancouver Island, which significantly brightened coastal waters. This phenomenon was observable from space due to the sheer scale of the fish gathering.

title: What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for April 2026, highlighting Mercury at its brightest, the Lyrid meteor shower peak, and a new comet. The event focuses on celestial observations visible to the naked eye.

title: La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa
summary: NASA will unveil the completed Roman Space Telescope and hold a press conference on April 21, 2024, at Goddard Space Flight Center. The telescope is undergoing final pre-launch tests.

title: NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing
summary: NASA is set to unveil the completed Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and host a media briefing on April 21. The telescope has finished construction and is undergoing final prelaunch testing.

title: NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown
summary: NASA has initiated the launch countdown for the Artemis II Moon mission, which will send four astronauts around the Moon. The countdown begins approximately two days before the scheduled liftoff.

title: Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager
summary: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager, supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station by ensuring temperature-controlled science experiments are safely transported using portable coolers, freezers, and refrigerators. His team coordinates all cold stowage hardware, operations, and personnel for the full flight cycle.

title: Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find
summary: Arctic winter sea ice tied the record low for the second consecutive year, according to NASA and NSIDC scientists. On March 15, the ice extent reached 5.52 million square miles, nearly matching the 2025 peak.

title: NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova
summary: NASA's IXPE mission captured a new observation of the supernova RCW 86, enhancing the understanding of its structure by combining data with other X-ray telescopes. The image, released on March 24, 2026, provides a more complete picture of the supernova's features.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4838-4844: Wrapping Up the Boxwork Terrain
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover has completed a week of intense science and engineering activities on Mars, wrapping up its monthslong investigation of the Martian boxwork terrain. The rover's operations were led by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

title: La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna
summary: NASA announced coverage plans for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight around the Moon. The launch is targeted for no earlier than April 1, with a two-hour launch window.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company had planned to proceed with the launch but faced an unspecified issue.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to Starbase launch pads in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch frequency. The activity highlights the company's focus on improving operational efficiency and scalability.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made to the launch site after previous operations.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the crewed lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2 in preparation for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This marks another milestone toward the next launch attempt.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for the week, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches are part of a busy period in the space industry.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details of the news were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, aiming to inform industry about upcoming activities and opportunities.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA is set to release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, which will be mandatory for all suppliers' financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna for satellite navigation that is resistant to interference and cost-effective to produce. The football-sized antenna offers a lightweight alternative to bulky, expensive existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments using ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates microgravity conditions for testing robotic and mobility systems. This initiative aims to foster hands-on learning and innovation in space technology among young researchers.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's evolving vision for Commercial Low-Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has sparked confusion and debate among policymakers.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to traditional space-based interceptors (SBIs). It is presented as a potential solution explored in the space industry.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions to streamline regulatory oversight. This plan aims to address gaps in mission authorization frameworks for unconventional space activities.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses storable bipropellants to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for space missions. The company aims to address the growing demand for spectrum management as more missions enter orbit.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has outlined a new plan to prioritize a commercially driven lunar economy with surface infrastructure. This marks a significant shift in NASA's strategic priorities.

title: One-On-One With SDA’s GP Sandhoo
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) has paused operations on its first operational satellite tranche due to minor issues, delaying benefits for troops. SDA Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo discussed the 'strategic pause' in an interview with Payload.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio Hosted Payload Platform
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio hosted payload platform, a new model where satellites stay in orbit while payloads can be added or removed as needed. This innovation aims to provide flexible and cost-effective solutions for payload hosting in space.

title: Ramon.Space, Ingrasys Aim To Fly Prototype Orbital Data Center In 2027
summary: Ramon.Space and Ingrasys are collaborating to launch a prototype orbital data center in 2027, joining the growing competition in space-based data processing. The initiative aims to leverage space environments for advanced computing and data storage.

title: Beyond the Rocket: The Digital Infrastructure of the Artemis II Mission
summary: The Artemis II mission's digital infrastructure, though unseen, is critical to its success, marking a significant advancement over Apollo-era technology. NASA and its partners, including Booz Allen, emphasize the complexity and modernization of the mission's digital systems.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating a rapidly deployable end-to-end ground system. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations and improve efficiency in the space industry.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The post highlights insights shared at the AvL booth.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market outlook amid volatility, with discussions focusing on direct-to-direct (D2D) communications, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discussed the company's RF solutions, including phased arrays for LEO satellites, during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Qorvo's advancements in satellite communication technology.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are prioritizing sovereign control over their satellite communications networks due to rising geopolitical tensions. The challenge lies in defining what sovereign space capabilities entail and their role in a complex global space infrastructure.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Venture capitalists at a SATShow panel highlighted the need for improved financing routes, including IPOs, to support the growing commercial space sector. They noted that financing has not kept pace with the industry's rapid technological advancements.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking data collected in space to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have adopted inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard competitive offering for passengers over the past two decades, with IFC developers now focusing on expanding and enhancing these networks to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are identified as key trends shaping the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators is expected to drive growth.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems at SATShow Week, featuring live multi-orbit demonstrations.
